How To Have A Purposeful Retirement While Also Having Money
It is rare for one retirement story to sound exactly the same as another. Many of them may follow the same trajectory, but the details tend to differ. For different people, the meaning of retirement is different. The meaning of retirement for an individual mainly depends on their life experiences, their approach towards money and financial matters, and what they expect from their retirement.
Even when such may be the case, people tend to have a few common expectations from their life after retirement. They want to live healthily and have a happy life after retirement, without having to worry about their finances, their basic necessities, or the medical needs of themselves and their partner. Some people may also seek more, such as fulfilling the needs of their family, especially their children. This is especially a concern with people who seek to support their children’s education or marriage.
All of these goals can be achieved with the right approach to saving for retirement. But what is retirement beyond these life goals?
Challenges of Retirement
People arrive at the threshold of retirement after having worked hard all their lives. Many of them tend to have some expectations from retirement. Beyond the basics, expect their superannuation to be a time of leisure they can spend with their loved ones, doing the things they enjoy.
However, many do not indulge in retirement planning. Neither do they plan for the basics, nor do they create a map for how they want to make the ideal use of their time and energy after retirement. Without a thorough retirement plan, one may end up feeling lost after they are done with their working days.
Traditionally, happy retirement is a phase of your life where you are not expected to work. Thus, you are no longer earning money actively. Moreover, you are not engrossed in any full-time work either. A situation like this, approached without understanding the meaning of retirement and a sound plan to face the same, leaves you with two things to worry about –
Firstly, you will have no active income.
This means you will have to live within your limited means or savings unless you have created savings for retirement in one form or another.
Secondly, you may end up feeling lost without a purpose.
To avoid feeling like this, it is important to chalk out some life goals that may help you stay focused on various aspects of your life.
Retiring with a Purpose
To avoid disappointments and disenchantments after retirement, it is essential to engage in retirement planning during your working years. As you may have guessed, there are two aspects that you need to focus on when planning. First, is to get your finances in order, and second, is to plan how you want your post-retirement life to be.
Both these aspects can be organised in cohesion. You need to plan today to ensure that after retirement, you have enough savings and ample income to live comfortably to fulfil all your dreams and needs. To achieve this, you need to envision how you want your post-retirement life to be. Do you want to live in a different home, or do you see yourself travelling often? Would you be pursuing any hobbies, or will you choose to turn an interest into an income source?
Once you have these details defined, you can also include the basics in your plan, such as medical needs, familial responsibilities, if any, and more. Going through these details will help you get an idea of how much money will you need to have a comfortable retirement. Then, you may use a retirement calculator to understand how and how much you need to start saving today, to reach your retirement goals.
Ensuring a Successful Retirement
Here are a few simple retirement saving tips to ensure a purposeful, successful retirement.
Get a retirement plan
Whether you get a pension plan, an annuity plan, or a ULIP, it is advisable to invest some of your money into a sound retirement plan, so that you may receive returns that allow you to have a comfortable retirement. You can browse for plans, compare them to select the one that suits you, and then use the corresponding retirement calculator to get estimates for the returns you can expect.
Get medical insurance
Have some form of medical cover for yourself as well as your partner. Medical needs tend to increase in the later years of life, and medical costs keep increasing year after year. Having a health cover can help you keep the financial impact of healthcare costs to a minimum.
Buy life insurance
Life insurance is another form of insurance you should get if you have dependents. It is ideal to buy this in your early years, as premium costs are lower then. If the costs are a concern, you can opt for term life insurance, which is known to be the affordable type of life cover.
Set goals
It is not ideal to step into retirement without knowing what you want from this phase of life. Doing so may leave you feeling lost and purposeless. Instead, set goals and have a vision for your future. You can seek the help of a retirement coach to do the same.
Have hobbies
Even if you do not have grand plans for your retirement, avoid approaching it with disinterest. Add some appeal to your post-retirement days by devoting this time to hobbies you have always wanted to practice or skills you have always wanted to learn.
Be socially active
Use this time to connect with friends and family. This should help you maintain a sense of purpose as well as lend you the feeling of having a community or a support system.
Stepping into retirement can be overwhelming. You can make it more pleasant by having some goals along with a plan to help you achieve these goals. These retirement savings tips should help you approach superannuation without fear and live through it purposefully.
